mechanism_narrative: Declining federal funds rate over a 3-month window signals monetary easing that reduces equity discount rates and increases present value of future corporate cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 5.33% as of 2024-08-01 suggests potential easing cycle ahead
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns following FEDFUNDS declines are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate rising more than 0.3 percentage points over a 3-month period signals labor market deterioration and recession risk, triggering equity risk-off behavior and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 4.2% as of 2024-08-01 provides baseline for deterioration detection
regime_hint: labor market deterioration or early recession signal
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 55% of triggered instances despite unemployment deterioration signal, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts decline by more than 10% quarter-over-quarter, signaling weakening residential demand and forward-looking economic contraction, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days as the market reprices growth expectations.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 1265.0 (revised) as of 2024-07-01 establishes baseline for deterioration measurement
regime_hint: demand-side contraction or housing cycle downturn
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are positive in > 50% of instances where HOUST declines > 10% QoQ, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production index rises above its 12-month moving average while remaining below trend, indicating supply-side capacity utilization improvement without inflationary pressure, supporting positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 99.9757 (revised) as of 2024-07-01 shows recent weakness relative to 2017=100 baseline
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are negative in > 50% of instances meeting the trigger condition, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Real retail sales growth accelerates above 2% annualized rate while unemployment remains stable, signaling robust consumer demand and earnings growth expectations, jointly driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days. The mechanism requires both demand strength and labor market stability to activate.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS growth > 2% annualized AND UNRATE change < 0.2pp in 3 months
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 698835.0 (revised) and UNRATE at 4.2% as of 2024-08-01
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are negative in > 55% of instances where both conditions are met, or the interaction term coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.05) in any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Inflation measured by CPI rises above 3% annualized rate while federal funds rate remains below 4%, creating negative real rates that compress equity risk premiums and drive positive S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days as investors seek real asset protection.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 314.062 (revised) and FEDFUNDS at 5.33% as of 2024-08-01
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are negative in > 50% of instances where both conditions hold, or the interaction effect is not statistically significant (p > 0.05) in any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment growth decelerates below 100,000 jobs per month over a 3-month rolling average while unemployment rate remains below 4.5%, signaling labor market cooling without recession, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days as growth expectations compress.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 157757.0 (revised) and UNRATE at 4.2% as of 2024-08-01
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are positive in > 55% of instances where both conditions are met, or the interaction coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.05) in any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth decelerates to below 1.5% annualized rate in the most recent quarter while housing starts remain above 1.2 million annualized units, indicating demand-side weakness despite housing resilience, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days as the market reprices recession probability.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 QoQ annualized growth < 1.5% AND HOUST > 1.2M annualized
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 23286.508 (revised) and HOUST at 1265.0 (revised) as of 2024-07-01
regime_hint: demand divergence or soft landing failure regime
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are positive in > 50% of instances where both conditions hold, or the interaction term is not statistically significant (p > 0.05) in any 3-year rolling window.
